questions.question_html: What has Bharat always been known for?
questions.solution_html: Bharat has always been known worldwide as a land of wise and heroic individuals.

questions.question_html: What is attractive about Bharat?
questions.solution_html: Bharat's rich and diverse culture has attracted numerous travellers since ancient times.

questions.question_html: What is special about the fact that the people live here in unison? Answer in the context of the chapter Hamara Bharat - Incredible India!.
questions.solution_html: The fact that the people live in unison presents to the world the concept of unity in diversity, which is special and noteworthy.

questions.question_html: What geographical features does the passage mention?  Answer in the context of the chapter Hamara Bharat - Incredible India!.
questions.solution_html: The passage mentions that Bharat is rich with rivers, lakes, and high mountains, and has green forests home to many plants, insects, birds, and animals.

questions.question_html: What is the advice given to everyone? Answer in the context of the chapter Hamara Bharat - Incredible India!.
questions.solution_html: The advice given is that as inhabitants of Bharat, people need to continue working hard and ensure that it prospers.

sections.label: Let us do these activities before we read.

questions.question_html:
Our country is beautiful and every state has its own uniqueness. The words given below are part of our identity. Complete these words with the correct vowels (A, E, I, O, U).

  1. We like to decorate our homes with our f _ l k _ rt.
  2. Our c _ lt _ re consists of our ideas, customs, traditions, values, arts, and languages.
  3. All of us celebrate our f _ st _ v _ ls together.
  4. We tell stories to our children to teach them about our c _ lt _ re, h _ st _ ry, and l _ g _ nds.
  5. We have our own l _ ng _ ag _ s to talk to each other.
questions.solution_html:
1. folk art
  2. culture
  3. festivals
  4. culture, history, legends
  5. languages

questions.question_html: What is the Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme all about?
questions.solution_html: The Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme is all about knowing that we all belong to one nation, sharing a common bond, and celebrating our unity in diversity.

questions.question_html: Why is this programme being conducted? Answer in the context of the chapter Hamara Bharat - Incredible India!.
questions.solution_html: This programme is being conducted to emphasize and promote the idea that our unity in diversity makes us the most wonderful country in the world.

questions.question_html:
On the basis of what the speakers said about the art forms, complete the table given below.

Name of the child State Art form Things needed
Akanksha   Aipan  
Priyaranjan     clay, brass scrap, wax, fire
Chitra      
Balamurali Andhra Pradesh Kondapalli
questions.solution_html:
Name of the child State Art form Things needed
Akanksha Uttrakhand Aipan White, rice flour paste, brick-red geru, floors, walls
Priyaranjan Odisha Dhokra clay, brass scrap, wax, fire
Chitra Kerala Coconut shell Brown coconut shell, sandpaper/machine, base, wood polish
Balamurali Andhra Pradesh Kondapalli Soft wood, tamarind seed powder, sawdust, oil, water-colours, vegetable dyes, enamel paints

questions.question_html: The designs for Aipan are taken from ________ and ________.
Dhokra metal craft is very old because it is ________ old.
A base is needed for coconut shell craft to make it ________.
Soft wood, ________ and colours are used to make Kondapalli toys.
Why does Akanksha’s family make Aipan?
questions.solution_html: Akanksha's family makes Aipan as part of family functions and festivals, decorating floors and walls, especially in puja rooms and outside the main door of their home.

questions.question_html: In Dhokra, why does wax come out of the small openings? Answer in the context of the chapter Hamara Bharat- Incredible India!
questions.solution_html: In Dhokra, wax comes out of the small openings when the figure covered with clay is put in the fire. The heat melts the wax, causing it to flow out, creating an empty space inside the clay mold for the liquid brass to be poured in.

questions.question_html: What is common in the toys made in Balamurali’s village and coconut shell craft in Kerala?
questions.solution_html: Both the toys made in Balamurali's village and the coconut shell craft in Kerala involve traditional, handmade processes and materials sourced from nature. Additionally, both crafts focus on creating items that reflect their cultural heritage and are often used for decorative or practical purposes.

questions.question_html:
Priyaranjan, Chitra and Balamurali share how art works of their states are made. They use some words to show the order of making it. Those words are given in the box below.

to begin, first, then, after that, next, finally, at last

Now, use some of these words to complete the paragraph given below. Use one word only once.

How I Get Ready for School

(i) ________ I get up and go to take a shower. (ii) ________, I wear my school dress. (iii) ________, I eat my food. (iv) ________, I wear my shoes. (v) ________, I pick up my bag and go out of my home.
questions.solution_html:
1. First
  2. After
  3. Then
  4. Next
  5. Finally

questions.question_html: The children from different states each speak about one art form from their state. When they speak, they use the first form of the verb. It shows something that happens regularly. It is called the present tense. With he, she and it, -s/-es is used with the verb to show the present tense. Make five sentences with the help of the words given in the table below. Use -s/-es wherever necessary. Two examples are given.

Listen to the description of gakkad bharta and complete the notes given below. (refer to page 162 for transcript)

Gakkad Bharta

  1. Name of the speaker: ________
  2. The town of the speaker: ________
  3. The state he belongs to: ________
  4. The name of the food item: ________
  5. Vegetables roasted for bharta: ________
questions.solution_html:
1. Name of the speaker: sushil
  2. The town of the speaker: Jabalpur
  3. The state he belongs to: Madhya Pradesh
  4. The name of the food item: Gakkad Bhartta
  5. Vegetables roasted for bharta: Brinjals and tomatoes

questions.question_html:
Now, listen to the description again and complete the flowchart on how to make gakkad bharta.

  1. To make gakkad, make balls of ________ dough and ________ them on high heat.
  2. To make bharta, ________ brinjal and tomatoes and garnish with ________ onions, coriander, green chillies and salt.
  3. To serve, crumble the gakkad, put some ________ on it and add one spoon of ghee.
questions.solution_html:
1. wheat, roast
  2. roast, chopped
  3. bharta

questions.question_html:
Speak about a local art form of your region. It can be any one out of painting, rangoli, metal craft, embroidery, music, dance or drama. Remember to include:

  • the name of the art form
  • what things do they need for it
  • how do they make/present it
questions.solution_html:
I know about the art form called Pattachitra. Pattachitra is a traditional painting style from Odisha, India.
They need cloth, natural pigments, and a fine brush. The cloth is first treated with a mixture of chalk and gum to create a smooth surface for painting.
To make it, first, they prepare the cloth by coating it with the chalk and gum mixture and let it dry. After that, they sketch the outlines of the design using a fine brush. Next, they fill in the colours using natural pigments derived from minerals and plants. Then, they add intricate details and patterns to the painting. At last, they apply a layer of lacquer to protect the painting and give it a glossy finish.
Pattachitra paintings often depict mythological stories, folk tales, and religious themes, with vibrant colours and detailed ornamentation, making them a unique and cherished art form of Odisha.

questions.question_html:
You may use the words given below when you speak.

  • I know about the art form called ...
  • They need ...
  • To make it, first ... after that ... next ... then ... at last ...
questions.solution_html:
I know about the art form called Kathakali. Kathakali is a traditional dance-drama from Kerala, India. They need elaborate costumes, makeup, musical instruments, and a stage.
To make it, first, the performers put on their elaborate costumes, which include brightly coloured skirts and large headdresses. After that, they apply intricate makeup to their faces, using natural pigments to create vibrant designs that represent different characters. Next, they prepare the stage and arrange the musical instruments, which include drums, cymbals, and a vocalist who narrates the story. Then, the performers enact scenes from mythological stories, using precise hand gestures, facial expressions, and body movements to convey the emotions and actions of the characters. At last, the performance concludes with a dramatic climax, leaving the audience in awe of the storytelling and artistry, Kathakali is unique because it combines dance, drama, music, and elaborate costumes to tell epic stories, making it a captivating and cherished art form of Kerala.

questions.question_html:
You heard children from different states speak about the art forms from their state. Discuss in pairs which art form you liked the most.
Now, write a paragraph with five sentences giving your reasons for liking it.
questions.solution_html: I liked the art form called Dhokra from Odisha the most. This ancient metal craft, practiced for over 4000 years, showcases incredible skill and tradition. The process of creating a Dhokra figure, from shaping the clay to pouring molten brass, is fascinating and intricate. The resulting sculptures are beautifully detailed and reflect the rich cultural heritage of the tribe. Additionally, the fact that each piece is handmade and unique makes Dhokra art truly special and a remarkable example of traditional craftsmanship.

questions.question_html:
Madhubani painting is a traditional art form from Bihar. The artists use natural colours to make them on the walls of their houses. You can also make and use natural colours as given below.

  • yellow from haldi
  • green from leaves
  • red from red flowers/geru/sindoor
  • orange from flowers
  • blue from neel/indigo powder

Given below is a Madhubani painting. Colour it with natural colours or any other colour.
questions.solution_html: Just do like the following picture:

questions.question_html:
Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat Programme
Bharat has diverse languages, cuisines, music, dance, theatre, movies and films, handicrafts, sports, literature, festivals, paintings, sculptures, etc. The Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat programme aims to enhance interaction and promote mutual understanding between people of different states/UTs through the concept of state/UT pairing. This is to celebrate the unity in diversity and strengthen the emotional bonds between its citizens. The vision is to enable people to develop a sense of common identity by sharing best practices and experiences. The programme aims to contribute to nation building by highlighting the inter-connectedness between various cultures and traditions.
questions.solution_html: The Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat Programme enhances interaction and mutual understanding between different states/UTs of India. It celebrates unity in diversity by fostering emotional bonds and sharing cultural practices. The initiative aims to build a common identity and contribute to nation-building through cultural interconnectedness and shared experiences.
